title: Recurring DB lock contention matches a prior incident
summary: orders-pg lock contention from a reporting batch recurs and degrades checkout, with the same signature as a prior incident stored in warm-layer memory (prior:inc-0603). The value here is recognizing the recurrence: the gold evidence links the current anomaly to the stored prior incident and surfaces its known mitigation, shortening time-to-resolution.
question: Why is checkout slow again at 03:05 on 2026-06-18, and have we seen this before?

ground_truth.notes: Same lock-contention signature as a stored prior incident. A correct bundle includes a previous_incident evidence item linking to prior:inc-0603 and recommends its recorded mitigation (reschedule the batch off-peak).
